summary: Security audit of Task 4 round 1 (commit 204886e): CrashReportActivity.kt and RestartCrashBanner.kt. No critical or major issues. Activity reads no intent extras, uses SAF with a clock-derived filename (no attacker-controllable input on the launch path), and never logs sensitive content. Hardcoded secrets: none. OWASP A01/A03/A05/A08/A09 exposure is limited by design (:crash process isolation is deferred to Task 5 manifest, which is correct scoping). Three minor findings surface defense-in-depth improvements (FLAG_SECURE on the crash screen, Toast context leak pattern, orphan crash.log if the delete step is cancelled between writeTo and delete) plus one informational Task-5 manifest reminder.

findings.title: CrashReportActivity does not set FLAG_SECURE
findings.description: The crash screen is a recovery UI that will be shown to the user immediately after a crash and can appear in the Recents overview and in any active screen-capture / screen-recording / screen-mirroring session. The screen itself currently renders only a localized title, body, and two buttons — so the leak surface is small. However, per Decision 11 the exported `.txt` that follows the Save button is deliberately unredacted during Phase 2.5, and a user who chose SAF target from the recents thumbnail may also have OEM screenshot-on-error collectors enabled. Adding `window.setFlags(FLAG_SECURE, FLAG_SECURE)` in `onCreate` is a cheap defense-in-depth that prevents the activity from appearing in screenshots / Recents thumbnails / non-trusted displays without changing any behaviour.
findings.suggested_fix: In `onCreate`, before `setContent { ... }`, add `window.setFlags(WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_SECURE, WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_SECURE)`. Import `android.view.WindowManager`. No user-visible change, Recents thumbnail and screenshots go blank.

findings.owasp: A08 Software and Data Integrity Failures
findings.title: Orphan crash.log if coroutine is cancelled between writeTo() and delete()
findings.description: `handleSafResult` sequence is `buildExport` → `writeTo(uri, content)` → `File(filesDir, "logs/crash.log").delete()` → Toast → finish(). If the user rotates / the activity is destroyed between the successful `writeTo` and the `delete`, the `lifecycleScope` coroutine is cancelled and the `delete()` + `finish()` are skipped, while the SAF file is already written. On next cold start `CrashState.hasUnresolvedCrash` is still true (crash.log still exists) and the banner re-appears even though the user already saved the log. This is not a security vulnerability per se (no data exposure, no integrity compromise of the saved file), but it does leave `:crash`-process state inconsistent with the user-visible outcome and can cause redundant exports. Note the task spec (Edge cases) explicitly accepts `.delete()` returning false silently — this finding is about the delete being SKIPPED, not returning false.
findings.suggested_fix: Make the success path resilient to cancellation between write and delete. Option A: call `File(...).delete()` inside a `withContext(NonCancellable)` block right after `writeTo` returns, before the Toast. Option B: move the delete to a terminal `finally` branch gated by a `written = true` flag set immediately after `writeTo` returns. Either option keeps crash.log cleanup atomic with the SAF write from the user's perspective.

findings.title: Toast uses Activity context instead of applicationContext
findings.description: Both `Toast.makeText(this, ...)` calls pass the Activity as context. If the activity is destroyed (rotation, user tapping Close concurrently, system reclaim) before the Toast's internal window token is consumed, the Toast can hold a reference to the destroyed Activity and on some OEM implementations log a BadTokenException. Using `applicationContext` is the Android-recommended pattern for Toasts and avoids any Activity-leak window. Low severity: Toast is short-lived and the :crash process is already going to be killed soon after finish(), but the pattern is trivially safer.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `Toast.makeText(this, R.string...., Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show()` with `Toast.makeText(applicationContext, R.string...., Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show()` in both branches of `handleSafResult`.

findings.title: Task 5 manifest reminder — CrashReportActivity must be exported=false
findings.description: The activity reads no intent extras and declares no intent filters, so it has no intent-based authentication surface — that is correct. However, Decision 3 / 5 and this task's contract rely on Task 5's manifest declaring `<activity android:name=".crash.CrashReportActivity" android:process=":crash" android:exported="false" ...>`. If Task 5 ships without `exported="false"` (or with any `<intent-filter>` that implicitly flips exported to true on targetSdk ≥ 31), any third-party app on the device could `startActivity` into this screen, mount a UI-redress / log-exfiltration attempt by invoking it at arbitrary times, and — via a user-consented SAF dialog — induce an export of unredacted device/crash state to an attacker-chosen location. Surfacing here as a Task-5 gate, per audit scope instructions.
findings.suggested_fix: In Task 5's `AndroidManifest.xml` change, explicitly set `android:exported="false"` on the `<activity>` entry for `app.sanctum.machina.crash.CrashReportActivity`. Add a grep-gate in Task 5's AC: `grep -n 'CrashReportActivity' app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` must show `android:exported="false"` on the same element, and there must be no `<intent-filter>` child.
